title: Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{MultipleIssues|redlink|cleanup}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:SuperReturnOfTheJedi.jpg]] |title=''Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' |developer=*[[LucasArts]] *[[Sculptured Software]] |publisher=*[[THQ]] *[[JVC|Victor]] (Japan) |release date=[[November]] [[1994]] {{C|[[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|SNES]], [[Wikipedia:Nintendo Game Boy|Game Boy]]}}{{LucasArts|url=20th/history_2.htm|text=20th Anniversary History, Part Two: The Classics, 1990 - 1994}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=[[Wikipedia:Action game|Action]] |modes=[[Wikipedia:Single-player|Single player]] |ratings=[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]: Everyone (E), [[Wikipedia:Entertainment and Leisure Software Publishers Association|ELSPA]]: 3+ |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|Super Nintendo Entertainment System]], [[Wikipedia:Nintendo Game Boy|Nintendo Game Boy]], [[Wikipedia:Wii|Virtual Console]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director=[[Kalani Streicher]]{{WebCite|url=https://archive.org/details/super-star-wars-return-of-the-jedi-usa-rev-1|text=''Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' Instruction Booklet|work=The Internet Archive|nobackup=1}} |producer= |designer=Kalani Streicher |programmer=[[Peter Ward]] |artist=[[Harrison Fong]] (Art Supervisor) |writer= |composer=[[Paul Webb]] (Based on [[John Williams]]) |timeline= |prev=[[Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] |next= }} '''''Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi''''', sometimes called '''''Super Return of the Jedi''''', is a platform game for the [[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|Super Nintendo Entertainment System]] (Super Famicom in Japan) and [[Wikipedia:Nintendo Game Boy|Nintendo Game Boy]] which follows ''[[Super Star Wars]]'' and ''[[Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back]]''. There is also a simplified version for the [[Wikipedia:Sega Game Gear|Sega Game Gear]] portable system. It is largely based on the third film of the original ''[[Star Wars]]'' trilogy, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Episode VI
RETURN OF THE JEDI |text=Luke Skywalker has returned to his home planet of Tatooine in an attempt to rescue his friend Han Solo from the clutches of the vile gangster Jabba the Hutt. Little does Luke know that the GALACTIC EMPIRE has secretly begun construction on a new armored space station even more powerful than the first dreaded Death Star. When completed, this ultimate weapon will spell certain doom for the small band of rebels struggling to restore freedom to the galaxy...}} ==Gameplay== ''Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' closely follows the standard set by the first two ''Super Star Wars'' games, with multiple playable characters and [[Wikipedia:Mode 7|Mode 7]] quasi-3D vehicle sequences. Controls are identical to the second game, and this installment also includes its predecessor's password save option. While the plot of the game roughly follows the film, several changes were made to accommodate gameplay. Characters fought their way from scene to scene and are confronted by multiple enemies. In addition to the standard [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], new playable characters include [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]] the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]. Bosses include the [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] door guard droid, the [[Rancor/Legends|Rancor]] beast, and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Vehicle sequences include the [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] chase, a ride to Jabba's Palace in the [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] and a cruise in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. ===Levels{{GalaxyCite|2|Super Return of the Jedi (article)}}=== [[File:Return_of_the_jedi_speeder.png|thumb|250px|A screenshot of the 3D [[Wikipedia:Mode 7|Mode 7]] sequence of the game where the player pilots a landspeeder to Jabba's Palace in the first level.]] #Tatooine 1 ([[Unidentified landspeeder|Landspeeder]]) #Tatooine 2 - Boss: [[TT-8L gatekeeper droid/Legends|Palace Door Guard]] #Jabba's Dance Hall - Boss: [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]] #Jabba's Palace - Boss: [[Frog-dog/Legends|Giant Frog-dog]] #Rancor Pit - Boss: [[Jabba's rancor/Legends|Rancor]] #Attack on Sail Barge - Boss: [[Esoomian/Legends|Esoomian]] #Inside Sail Barge - Boss: [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] #Endor - [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|Speeder Bikes]]''' #[[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Ewok Village]] A - Boss: Imperial [[EV-series supervisor droid/Legends|EV-series supervisor droid]] #Ewok Village B - Boss: [[Giant forest beast]] #Endor - Boss: Imperial Ship #Endor Space Battle (''Millennium Falcon'') #[[Endor shield generator bunker|Power Generator]] - Boss: [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|Deflector Shield Generator]] #Inside [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]] - Boss: [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper]] #Death Star Surface #Tower - Boss: [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]], Zero-G assault stormtrooper #Tower Entrance - Vader - Boss: [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] #[[Emperor's Throne Room/Legends|Emperor's Chamber]] - Boss: [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor Palpatine]] #Death Star Tunnel ===Enemies===
